topic: Two-pole geomagnetic model — third-horn closure (aetheric retreat) added to detail_evidence paragraph 2
change_summary:
EXP-219 integrated: single-sentence third-horn closure inserted into detail_evidence paragraph 2 immediately after "only a relabelling of it)." and before the "

A second self-contradiction" paragraph break. The new sentence (~86 words) names the metaphysical/aetheric-retreat as the defender's strongest remaining escape, then closes it by showing that waiving interior flux conservation unmoors A, C, and λ from the toroidal geometry and collapses B(r) into a surface fit (§6.1 'observed values relabeled'). TLDRs unchanged. detail_evidence grew from 7,310 → 8,126 chars (+816). Verdict, other paragraphs, and code_analysis tags unchanged. Decider corrected EXP-219's specified anchor (#p6-61-1) to the actually-resolving anchor (#p6-61-0) during integration — a necessary fix (no #p6-61-1 exists in the rendered HTML; tldr_verdict already uses #p6-61-0).
our_argument_summary: The dome's fitted two-pole B(r) equation violates flux conservation in its own claimed ring-magnet topology by a factor of ~1,600, exponential decay is inconsistent with what standard EM predicts for a wound toroid (1/r), and A, C, and λ are fitted to observed pole magnitudes rather than derived from disc_radius or firmament_height. EXP-194 (integrated in c7) closed the two charitable surface-vs-subterranean rescues. EXP-219 (this cycle) closes the third and strongest defender escape — the metaphysical/aetheric retreat that waives Maxwell entirely — by showing it sacrifices the toroidal derivation at the cost of restoring the paragraph's internal consistency.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: No pipeline. monitor.py does not contain any WIN-053-specific domain, and no function in the dome's automation computes B(r). The 20-domain live-power table (raw-text/08-live-power.txt) does not include a 'two-pole B(r)' or 'toroidal magnetic' domain. Consistent with c6/c7.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: N/A — the automation does not touch this WIN. The equation B(r) = 62,376·e^(−r_N/8619) + 64,852·e^(−r_S/8619) appears in dome prose only; the numerical values are neither computed nor checked at runtime.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: The dome model itself (not monitor.py) now has three prose-level escapes from the 1,600:1 flux contradiction: (1) narrow subterranean return channel, (2) globe-borrowed crustal bridging, (3) aetheric/metaphysical retreat. All three are explicitly disjoined and closed in the current detail_evidence paragraph 2.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None for this cycle. Prose now references '§6.1 observed values relabeled' as a forward claim about the §6.1 framing; verify no §6.1 drift breaks this cross-ref (see hole 3 below).

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Unchanged from c6/c7. No WIN-053 domain in monitor.py; no WIN-053-specific data fetch in pull_data.py; WIN-053 is not one of the 20 live-dashboard domains. The third-horn insertion is prose-only and does not introduce any code dependency.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Unchanged from c6/c7. Standard geomagnetism (IGRF-13 / CHAOS-7 / WMM2025, degree-13 spherical harmonic expansion grounded in geodynamo physics) predicts the full field including the SAA at ~50 nT global RMS. The dome's ~20% RMS (~10,000–13,000 nT error) is ~200× worse, and the dome's two-parameter exponential cannot reproduce any localized anomaly structure or secular variation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Unchanged from c6/c7. A=62,376 nT and C=64,852 nT align with observed NMP (~54–56k nT) and geographic south pole (~55k nT per WMM2025) magnitudes published for decades. PRED-TOROID-002 was registered 2026-04-04 with claimed confirmation date 2026-03-23 — confirmation predates registration, i.e. retrodiction registered after the fact.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Unchanged from c6/c7. No derivation of A, C, or λ from disc_radius (20,015 km) or firmament_height (9,086 km) exists on the dome site or in the repo. The scale length λ=8,619 km is shared between H(r) and B(r) — cross-domain parameter reuse, not a derivation. A tightly-wound solenoidal toroid gives B ∝ 1/r (Griffiths §5.3.3), not e^(−r/λ); the exponential form is asserted, not derived. The c8 third-horn insertion reinforces this conclusion by showing that even in the most favourable defender stance (aetheric retreat), the dome has not specified a derivation path.

holes_found.description: "PARAMETERLESS SURFACE FIT" IS TECHNICALLY IMPRECISE. The inserted sentence says "B(r) demotes to a parameterless surface fit to observed pole magnitudes." But B(r) has three explicit parameters — A=62,376 nT, C=64,852 nT, and λ=8,619 km — all three are literally present in the equation. The intended meaning is "no dome-geometry-derived parameters" (i.e. the parameters are fitted rather than derived), which the surrounding context partially clarifies ("no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry"). But the literal phrase "parameterless" is self-contradictory with paragraph 4 two paragraphs later which explicitly names A and C as fitted coefficients. A sharp defender quoting this single sentence out of context can ridicule our review for calling a three-parameter equation "parameterless."
holes_found.recommendation: Tighten the phrase to something that is literally accurate. Options: (a) "B(r) demotes to a three-parameter surface fit to observed pole magnitudes — none of A, C, or λ derived from dome geometry"; (b) "B(r) demotes to a fitted-to-surface-observations curve with no dome-geometry-derived parameters"; (c) simply "B(r) demotes to a curve fit of observed pole magnitudes." Option (a) is the most informative and stays closest to the sentence's rhetorical shape. Single-sentence edit; low risk.

holes_found.description: "THE TOROIDAL GEOMETRY DOES NO PREDICTIVE WORK" IS OVER-STRONG. A defender reading this last clause can respond: "The toroidal architecture does predict something — that B(r) is a sum of two terms, one from each pole, rather than the single monopolar e^(−r/λ) of V50.6. That is a functional-form prediction, not zero predictive work. You are conflating 'no parameter values derived' with 'no predictive content at all.'" The preceding clause "no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry" already makes the correct (narrower) point. The final clause generalizes to "no predictive work," which overshoots.
holes_found.recommendation: Either delete the final "and the toroidal geometry does no predictive work" clause, or narrow it — e.g. "...a curve fit, and the toroidal geometry does no numerical predictive work beyond dictating that the fit must have two peaks." The latter preserves the rhetorical closure while being defensible under adversarial quotation. I do NOT recommend escalating to major because (a) the preceding clauses contain the load-bearing argument, and (b) EXP-219's first anticipated_objection acknowledges exactly this critique and is right that the specific predictive contribution (two-peak topology) is both weak and entangled with unfalsifiable aether-to-matter coupling, but that nuance is not in the rendered prose.

holes_found.description: THE §6.1 CROSS-REFERENCE IS LOAD-BEARING ON A SPECIFIC PHRASE. The new sentence packages the closure as "the 'observed values relabeled' failure mode documented in §6.1." The exact phrase "observed values relabeled" is not the §6.1 section heading (which is "Zero Fitted Parameters — The Hidden Parameters") and is not a verbatim phrase in the §6.1 body text. The closest phrase actually rendered on-page is Section 6.4's title "Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions." If the dome author or a third-party reviewer clicks the §6.1 link expecting to find the "observed values relabeled" framing, they will find a different (though related) argument about scale-length-reuse plus hidden amplitude/coupling parameters. This is a minor honesty-of-cross-reference issue, not a factual error.
holes_found.recommendation: Two acceptable fixes: (a) change the §6.1 anchor in the new sentence to §6.4 (which DOES use the "Globe Values Relabeled" phrasing verbatim), while keeping §6.1 as the hidden-parameters citation elsewhere; (b) reword the parenthetical to match §6.1's actual framing — e.g. "(the same hidden-fitted-parameters critique §6.1 levels at the λ=8,619 km scale length reuse)." Option (b) preserves the §6.1 link and adjusts the phrase; option (a) preserves the phrase and updates the link. Either is fine. Not blocking.

holes_found.description: STILL-OPEN H1 FROM c7 (MAJOR): Internal inconsistency on the southern comparator between paragraph 2 ("~55,000 nT measured at the geographic south pole") and paragraph 3 ("A=62,376 and C=64,852 match ~54,000–56,000 nT at the North Magnetic Pole and ~55,000 nT at the geographic south pole"). EXP-219 was explicitly scoped to H3 (metaphysical retreat) only and does not address H1. The EXP's unaddressed_holes_note acknowledges this. H1 remains a priority for a separate EXP — it reads as more pressing than the H3 fix we just made, because a dome defender can point at it without needing to reach the third horn. Recommend decider queues a dedicated EXP for H1 before the next holistic pass on WIN-053.
holes_found.recommendation: Open (or resurface if already tracked) an issue for H1. The c7 review's Option A recommendation is the correct fix: rewrite paragraph 3 to consistently use "geographic south pole" (not "North Magnetic Pole") as the comparator, matching paragraph 2. This is a single-sentence edit in detail_evidence paragraph 3.

holes_found.description: C7 H2 (MODERATE, "EXPLICITLY INTERMAGNET / CHAOS-7" OVERCLAIM) is now RESOLVED. Paragraph 2 currently reads "the surface-observatory sources the dome's own home page highlights (INTERMAGNET ground stations and CHAOS-7, which the dome mischaracterizes as 'paleomagnetic' but which is actually a present-day satellite-and-observatory composite)" — this matches the c7-recommended rewording. No further action for H2.
holes_found.recommendation: No action — recording that H2 is resolved for the decider's c7→c8 diff.

holes_found.description: STILL-OPEN TRACKED ITEMS (no new action needed this cycle): ISS-1243 (c5 H3, symmetric-Sump-aperture rescue), ISS-1244 (c5 H6, 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row absent from parenthetical — still only 500 km and 1,000 km considered), ISS-1245 (c5 H7, source-vs-sink polarity — both A and C positive-signed, topologically a monopole pair not a source-sink dipole), ISS-1246 (c5 H8, Sub-H(r) δ=6,371 km unsourced in detail_extra), c7 H4 (mantle-vs-rock imprecision), c7 H5 (crustal-conduction label imprecision). All six are tracked and EXP-219 is explicitly out-of-scope for them.
holes_found.recommendation: No new action; tracked issues will be addressed by future EXPs.

stronger_arguments.description: CONSIDER EXPLICITLY LINKING THE AETHERIC-RETREAT HORN TO WIN-012's κ-coupling collapse. The existing detail_evidence paragraph 5 already cross-references WIN-012 for the parallel κ coupling collapse. The new third-horn sentence does not mention that the aetheric-retreat move has already been tried for κ (WIN-012) and failed by the same "retreat unmoors the derivation" mechanism. A single parenthetical ("a pattern already established in WIN-012's κ collapse") would draw the reader's attention to the fact that this is the second time the dome has used the aetheric retreat to escape a quantitative contradiction — suggestive of a systemic rather than local failure mode.
sources: Existing cross-reference in WIN-053 paragraph 5: "See also WIN-012 for the parallel κ coupling collapse"

stronger_arguments.description: CONSIDER ADDING A UNIT-CALIBRATION BITE (EXP-219's deeper_analysis mentioned but deferred). Nanoteslas are defined via the Lorentz force law F = q·v×B, which is Maxwellian. Reporting B(r) in SI nT units while disclaiming Maxwell for the interior is self-defeating: the surface value 64,852 nT is only meaningful under Maxwell, so the retreat either (a) must restate the equation in aetheric units (which the dome has not specified) or (b) concedes the whole SI-nT framing and thereby concedes the surface-observatory comparison that underlies the 61%→20% RMS claim. This is stronger than the current sentence for the subset of readers who follow the unit chain. Not a must-add — the EXP explicitly considered and rejected it for scope reasons — but worth keeping in the backlog for a future clean-up pass on paragraph 2.
sources: EXP-219 deeper_analysis (unit-calibration argument explicitly considered and deferred)
SI definition of the tesla via F = q·v×B (Maxwellian)

kernel_of_truth.description: The metaphysical retreat IS a real move in physics. Modified-gravity programmes (MOND, TeVeS, bimetric), dark-sector models, and emergent-spacetime approaches all proceed by saying "standard equations don't apply in regime X; here is the replacement theory." The inserted sentence credits this respectfully ("the defender's strongest remaining move") which is the correct posture — waving it away dogmatically would make our review sound reactive. EXP-219's kernel_of_truth identification is exactly right.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The standard move's entry ticket is supplying the replacement theory (field equations, coupling laws, conserved quantities) — without it the retreat is not a theory but an evasion. The dome asserts B(r) in SI nanotesla units, fits it to observed WMM pole strengths, and claims toroidal architecture as its origin; once Maxwell is waived, the origin claim has nothing to stand on, the SI units are ambiguous, and A, C, λ become fitted parameters rather than geometric derivations. The retreat succeeds only by sacrificing the WIN's headline claim (that toroidal architecture predicts the dipolar field). The inserted sentence captures exactly this trade-off.

deeper_analysis:
EXP-219 was a surgical, well-scoped insertion that closes c7 H3 and (as a welcome side-effect) also addresses c7 H5 by embedding the §6.1 cross-reference directly in the new sentence. The third-horn closure reads naturally in context — it picks up cleanly from "only a relabelling of it)." and sets up the following "A second self-contradiction" paragraph without visible seam.

The two moderate holes above (parameterless-surface-fit imprecision; no-predictive-work over-strength) are genuine but small. They are NOT grounds for rollback — both are minor-edit fixes to the inserted sentence. The inserted sentence is net-positive: it acknowledges and closes the defender's strongest escape, which is more important than the minor imprecisions in its closing clauses.

Anchor note: the decider correctly overrode EXP-219's specified #p6-61-1 anchor to the actually-resolving #p6-61-0. The analyst's notes_for_decider and deeper_analysis both referred to #p6-61-1 as "the anchor already present in tldr_verdict," but that was mistaken — tldr_verdict uses #p6-61-0 (verified in docs/index.html), and no #p6-61-1 anchor exists in the rendered site. The decider's silent fix was correct; the analyst should be alerted so the miscalibration doesn't reoccur in EXP-220+. Decider could log this as a low-priority note to analyst.

On the other side of the argument (user preference, applied):

1. A dome defender might argue that labelling the aetheric retreat as "the defender's strongest remaining move" is strategically suboptimal for our case — by ranking defender moves, we implicitly concede that the dome has at least three credible defences, which sounds like more than zero. The counter-case: credit foregrounds closure. If we call the retreat weak, the reader suspects we are stacking the deck; calling it the strongest makes the subsequent collapse more final. The analyst's kernel_of_truth discipline is right.

2. A defender might also argue that the inserted sentence does not actually engage the version of the retreat where aetheric flow produces a Maxwellian surface B field via an unspecified coupling at the crust (EXP-219 anticipated_objection #1). On that stance, ∇·B = 0 applies above the crust, there IS no "interior flux conservation" to waive (interior is aetheric, exterior is Maxwellian), and the 1,600:1 constraint (derived assuming a closed Maxwellian loop through the Sump) no longer applies. The on-page sentence says "interior flux conservation is waived, no derivation links A, C, or λ to the toroidal geometry" — this handles the case, because any coupling law would have to supply additional fitted parameters, widening the §6.1 critique. But the reader has to chain three steps to see it. A subsequent cleanup could make that chain explicit, but it is not urgent.

3. Against my own moderate hole on "parameterless surface fit": a charitable reading treats "parameterless" as a compound modifier meaning "having no dome-geometry-derived parameters," which the surrounding text clarifies. If the analyst wants to keep the sharp rhetorical phrase, "parameterless-with-respect-to-dome-geometry" would survive adversarial quotation better than plain "parameterless." Either way it is a minor edit, not a rollback.

Net: EXP-219 does what it was scoped to do. The residual imprecisions are worth one more surgical patch.

Verbatim phrase "Globe Values Relabeled as Dome Predictions" appears as Section 6.4 heading (supports the "relabeled" framing used in the new sentence).
tldr_verdict of WIN-053 already uses #p6-61-0 cross-ref — so the new sentence is internally consistent with the WIN's existing cross-reference style.
citations_failed: EXP-219's replacement_sentence specified anchor #p6-61-1; this does NOT exist in docs/index.html (only #p6-61-0, #p6-610-9, and #p6-611-10 exist). The decider correctly overrode this to #p6-61-0 during integration. Flagging for the analyst-feedback channel so this doesn't recur: the correct §6.1 anchor is #p6-61-0, not #p6-61-1.
